name: German Sauerbraten Coca-Cola
recipeIngredient: 4 pound Beef rump, sirloin tip, OR
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large plastic bag, combine vinegar, Coca-Cola, water, onions, celery, carrots, pepper, cloves, bay leaves, sugar, and salt. Pour over meat.
2. Fasten the bag tightly and lay flat in a 13x9-inch pan. Refrigerate, turning the bag each day. Let marinate for up to 4 days if desired.
3. When ready to cook, remove meat (saving marinade) and dry well. Rub the surface lightly with flour.
4. In a Dutch oven, heat oil or shortening and slowly brown the meat on all sides.
5. Add 1 cup of the marinade liquid plus some of the vegetables and bay leaves. Cover tightly and simmer on surface heat or in a preheated 350°F oven for 3 to 4 hours until the meat is fork-tender.
6. If needed, add more marinade during cooking to keep at least 1/2-inch liquid in the Dutch oven.
7. Remove the meat and keep warm until ready to slice.
GRAVY:
1. Strain the drippings into a large measuring cup. Add several ice cubes and let stand a few minutes until the fat separates out.
2. Remove the fat, then make gravy by combining the remaining ingredients in the Dutch oven. Stir and cook about 5 minutes over medium heat until gravy is thickened. Taste for seasonings.
3. Makes 8 servings.
TO MAKE THE GRAVY: In the Dutch oven, combine the gravy ingredients, stir and cook about 5 minutes over medium heat until gravy is thickened. Taste for seasonings. Makes 3 cups of gravy.
